name: Pillow Case Jerky
recipeInstructions:
1. Get as large a boneless butt roast as possible.
2. Cut out individual muscles from the meat and discard all fat and connective tissue.
3. Slice the meat across the grain as thin as possible (1/16 inch).
4. Season both sides of the meat generously with salt and pepper, using more pepper than salt.
5. Lay out the meat strips on a rack in an insert for a smoker or oven.
6. Cook at low heat for 8 to 10 hours, turning every 3 to 4 hours until dried.
7. Store in a pillowcase (not plastic) as the meat needs to breathe and avoid using a paper sack to prevent a paper taste.
